title: Access Request and Approval Completeness for CDE VPN Accounts

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928)
spl:
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" subtype="gateway-connected" earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_cde_vpn_profiles.csv portal OUTPUT cde_profile
| where cde_profile="true"
| eval user=lower(user)
| join type=left max=1 user [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" earliest=-30d short_description="*VPN*CDE*"
    | eval user=lower(caller_id)
    | stats latest(state) as req_state by user
  ]
| where isnull(req_state) OR NOT match(req_state,"(?i)closed|fulfilled")
| stats dc(_time) as connect_events by user, source
| sort - connect_events
value: Verifies each new VPN entitlement for CDE users has a matching approved access request record so access is granted only with documented business justification.
implementation: (1) Normalize `user` to corporate UPN. (2) Align RITM naming with join filter. (3) Alert IAM on missing ticket within 24h of first connect. (4) Monthly reconciliation report.
visualization: Table (users without tickets), column chart (connect_events), pie (fulfilled vs missing).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
